answer:
The Stripe Model Context Protocol (MCP) server enables AI agents to interact with Stripe APIs and documentation via standardized function calls [1][2]. Stripe hosts a remote server at https://mcp.stripe.com, which is the recommended method for most users, supporting OAuth for secure, granular authentication [2][3].

For local execution, you can run the server using npx:

```bash
npx -y @stripe/mcp --api-key=YOUR_STRIPE_SECRET_KEY
```

This server provides tools to search Stripe resources, perform read and write API operations, manage account info, and search documentation [2][4]. For Connect platforms, you can act on behalf of connected accounts by passing the --stripe-account flag or header [2][5].
